pr.title: Properly compare builds on push event
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the GitHub Actions build workflow so diff-mode build comparison handles push events correctly. The workflow now checks out the previous commit with HEAD^ on push events, keeps the existing fetch/checkout behavior for pull_request events, disables detached-head advice, and adds explicit failure branches for unsupported matrix modes or event names.
what_changed: In `.github/workflows/build.yml`, expanded the Git config step from a single command to a script block.
Added `git config --global advice.detachedHead false` to suppress detached HEAD advice during CI checkouts.
In the Build step's mode switch, explicitly allowed the `diff` mode and added a default `*) exit 1 ;;` branch for unknown modes.
Changed the diff comparison checkout logic to branch on `${{ github.event_name }}`.
For `pull_request` events, retained the previous behavior: `git fetch origin HEAD` followed by `git checkout FETCH_HEAD`.
For `push` events, added `git checkout 'HEAD^'` so the expected build is generated from the parent commit rather than refetching/checking out the current HEAD.
Added a default event-name guard that exits with failure for unsupported event types.
Added a default failure branch to the later mode switch that runs `ninja all_source` for diff mode.
smart_moves: Separated pull_request and push behavior instead of assuming `git fetch origin HEAD` means the same thing for both event types.
Used `HEAD^` for push events, which directly compares the pushed commit against its immediate parent in the local checkout.
Kept the pull_request path unchanged, reducing risk to the existing PR comparison behavior.
Added explicit `*) exit 1 ;;` cases so unsupported modes or event types fail loudly instead of silently running with bad assumptions.
Disabled detached-head advice to keep CI logs cleaner after intentional detached checkout operations.
decomp_lessons: CI diff checks for decomp progress must compare against the correct baseline commit; on push events, the correct baseline may be the parent commit rather than a fetched remote HEAD.
Workflow scripts that are shared across pull_request and push events should branch explicitly on `github.event_name` when Git history semantics differ.
Fail-fast defaults in shell `case` statements are useful for build infrastructure because silent fallthrough can hide invalid matrix modes or unsupported event contexts.
Small CI fixes can materially affect trust in matching/progress reports, because an incorrect baseline can make build comparisons misleading.
naming_conventions: The workflow uses matrix mode names such as `link` and `diff`.
The build comparison script uses `actual` for artifacts built from the current source and `expected` for artifacts built after checking out the comparison baseline.
The workflow uses `config_args` to accumulate arguments passed to `python configure.py`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly or source matching tactic was changed; this PR affected CI comparison behavior.
The relevant matching-adjacent tactic is ensuring `ninja diff`/build comparison uses the intended baseline so reported differences reflect real source changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
searchable_terms: Properly compare builds on push event
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `.github/workflows/build.yml` diff-mode behavior.
Search for CI failures or discussions involving `HEAD^`, `FETCH_HEAD`, or push-event build comparison.
Check whether subsequent workflow changes handle multi-commit pushes differently from single-commit pushes.
